title: Understand: The Fourteenth Amendment—An Overview
description: Check your understanding of the the Fourteenth Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Fourteenth Amendment: Citizenship, Freedom, and Equality

assessment_items.question_content:
An [[☃ definition 2]] 1857 Supreme Court ruling said that African Americans “had no rights that a white man was bound to respect.” 

**What was the name of the case in which this ruling was issued?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: well-known for being bad or disgraceful
well-known for being bad or disgraceful

assessment_items.question_content:
**The decision in *Dred Scott v. Sanford* (1857) asserted that African Americans \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: were not and could never be U.S. citizens

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which amendment helped ensure that the Declaration of Independence’s promise of freedom and equality became a reality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which clause in the Fourteenth Amendment addressed and overturned the core conclusion in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which statement best describes how the Reconstruction Amendments affected the powers of the national government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They expanded the powers of the national government over civil rights.
They restricted the powers of the national government.
They had no effect on the national government’s power over civil rights.
They eliminated all of the national powers over civil rights.
A strong form of judicial review that determines the unconstitutionality of laws when those laws discriminate or burden an important right
The power of courts to determine whether a law is constitutional and to strike down that law if it is not constitutional
The power of a court to hear and issue a judgment in a particular case
A document that is submitted by a person not involved in a case and that advises the court on a particular matter

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is a requirement for a state law to pass the rational-basis test?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The government’s actions must be related to a legitimate government interest.
The objections to the law must not be related to how the law is enforced.
The state legislature that passed the law must have been elected by a popular vote.
The law must not have received a veto at any stage of the lawmaking process.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which Supreme Court case led to the development of the level of review known as “strict scrutiny”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Why is the passage of the Fourteenth Amendment considered a “Second Founding” for America?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It removed the word *slavery* from the Constitution.
It enshrined the concept of “equality” in the Constitution.
It led to the admission of new states following the Civil War.
It defined the naturalization process for immigrants.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the purpose of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To give the President absolute power over all state laws
To give enslaved people their freedom and ban the institution of slavery
To give states the power to nullify federal laws that they disagree with
To give Congress the power to protect the rights of Black Americans

assessment_items.question_content:
**Who is considered to be a key architect of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Laws receiving rational basis review are usually  \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
